children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The provisions of section 2704 (other than subsections (d) and (f)) shall apply to health insurance coverage offered by a health insurance issuer in the individual market in the same manner as it applies to health insurance coverage offered by a health insurance issuer in connection with a group health plan in the small or large group market.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Notice requirement
children.label: b Notice requirement
children.text: A health insurance issuer under this part shall comply with the notice requirement under with respect to the requirements referred to in subsection (a) as if such section applied to such issuer and such issuer were a group health plan.section 1185(d) of title 29

children.heading: Preemption; exception for health insurance coverage in certain States
children.label: c Preemption; exception for health insurance coverage in certain States
A
children.text: Such State law requires such coverage to provide for at least a 48-hour hospital length of stay following a normal vaginal delivery and at least a 96-hour hospital length of stay following a cesarean section.

children.label: B
children.text: Such State law requires such coverage to provide for maternity and pediatric care in accordance with guidelines established by the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists, the American Academy of Pediatrics, or other established professional medical associations.

children.label: C
children.text: Such State law requires, in connection with such coverage for maternity care, that the hospital length of stay for such care is left to the decision of (or required to be made by) the attending provider in consultation with the mother.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 2 Construction
children.text: shall not be construed as superseding a State law described in paragraph (1).Section 300gg–62(a) of this title
